results.rendered_html:
piṣṭa (von piṣ)
1〉 adj. gemahlen u. s. w. s. u. piṣ.
— 2〉 m.
a〉 Gebäck s. u. piṣ.
— b〉 N. pr. eines Mannes gaṇa śivādi zu  P. 4,1,112 . pl. seine Nachkommen gaṇa upakādi zu  P. 2,4,69 .
— 3〉 n. Mehl; Blei s. u. piṣ.

results.rendered_html:
piṣṭa p. p. [piṣ-kta]
1 Ground, powdered, crushed;  Bv. 1. 12. 
2 Rubbed together, squeezed or clasped (as the hands).
3 Kneaded.
— ṣṭaṃ
1 Anything ground, a ground substance.
2 Flour, meal; piṣṭaṃ pinaṣṭi ‘he grinds flour’; i. e. does a useless
(pb) work or a profitless repetition.
3 Lead.
Comp.
— udakaṃ water mixed with flour.
— pacana a pan for parching flour, a boiler &c.
— paśuḥ an effigy of a beast made with flour.
— pākabhṛt m. a boiler
— pācakaṃ a boiler.
— piṃḍaḥ a cake or ball of flour.
— pūraḥ see ghṛtapūra-peṣaḥ, -peṣaṇaṃ ‘grinding flour’; i. e. doing any useless work, a vain or profitless repetition. ˚nyāyaḥ see under nyāya.
— mehaḥ a variety of diabetes.
— vartiḥ a kind of small ball made of the flour of barley, pulse or rice.
— saurabhaḥ (pounded) sandal wood.
